Welcome to '''Wiki Almanac''' — a mini encyclopedia of [[J. R. R. Tolkien]]'s [[Middle-earth]], powered by [[MediaWiki]].

== Featured Article ==
'''[[The Lord of the Rings]]''' is a high-fantasy epic novel by [[J. R. R. Tolkien]], following [[Frodo Baggins]] and the [[Fellowship of the Ring]] on a quest to destroy the [[One Ring]] and defeat [[Sauron]]. Published in 1954–1955, it has sold over 150 million copies and inspired generations of readers, writers, and filmmakers.

== Characters ==
* [[Frodo Baggins]] — Ring-bearer and hero of the quest
* [[Samwise Gamgee]] — Frodo's loyal companion
* [[Gandalf]] — the Grey Wizard, guide to the Fellowship
* [[Aragorn]] — heir of Isildur, King Elessar
* [[Legolas]] — Elven prince of Mirkwood
* [[Gimli]] — Dwarf warrior, son of Glóin
* [[Boromir]] — Man of Gondor, son of Denethor
* [[Gollum]] — the wretched former Ring-bearer
* [[Sauron]] — the Dark Lord
* [[Saruman]] — the corrupted wizard
* [[Galadriel]] — Lady of Lothlórien
* [[Théoden]] — King of Rohan
* [[Éowyn]] — Shieldmaiden of Rohan

== Places ==
* [[Middle-earth]] — the world of Tolkien's legendarium
* [[The Shire]] — homeland of the Hobbits
* [[Rivendell]] — Elrond's refuge
* [[Lothlórien]] — the golden forest
* [[Rohan]] — kingdom of the Horse-lords
* [[Gondor]] — southern kingdom of Men
* [[Minas Tirith]] — the White City
* [[Mordor]] — Sauron's realm

== Artifacts & Groups ==
* [[One Ring]] — the ruling Ring forged by Sauron
* [[Fellowship of the Ring]] — the nine companions
* [[Nazgûl]] — the Ringwraiths
* [[Balrog]] — ancient demons of fire and shadow

== Categories ==
* [[:Category:Characters]]
* [[:Category:Places]]
* [[:Category:Artifacts]]
* [[:Category:Works]]
* [[:Category:Hobbits]]
* [[:Category:Dwarves]]
* [[:Category:Wizards]]
* [[:Category:Istari]]
* [[:Category:Dúnedain]]

== Explore ==
* [[Special:AllPages]]
* [[Special:Categories]]

== About ==
This starter setup is meant to prove a topic wiki can come to life quickly using real MediaWiki pages, categories, talk pages, and special pages out of the box.
